thread.title: Hemant Soren is Jharkhand's choice for 2nd straight term. What worked for him?
thread.title_full: Hemant Soren is Jharkhand's choice for 2nd straight term. What worked for him?

author: @indiatoday
title: Hemant Soren is Jharkhand's choice for 2nd straight term. What worked for him?
text:
Hemant Soren is Jharkhand's choice for 2nd straight term. What worked for him?
The INDIA bloc, led by the Jharkhand Mukti Morcha, won 56 out of the 81 Assembly seats as per the elections results announced by the Election Commission on Saturday.
In Short
- Women's welfare schemes of Soren government proved major factor
- Campaign by Kalpana Soren established connect with voters
- NDA's lack of trust on local leadership played big role too
Elections results announced on Saturday evoked mixed feelings in the BJP camp, with the party registering a landslide victory in Maharashtra Assembly polls, but failing to make a lasting impact in Jharkhand by winning just 24 out of 81 seats in the state.
Jharkhand has set a new record of repeating an incumbent government. After the state was carved out of Bihar 24 years ago, no alliance or party could win the Assembly polls back-to-back.
[Chief Minister Hemant Soren](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/jharkhand-election-results-2024-hemant-soren-factor-jmm-congress-corruption-charges-2638687-2024-11-23?utm_source=global-search&utm_medium=global-search&utm_campaign=global-search)-led the INDIA bloc stunned its critics with an impressive performance in the elections, registering better numbers than 2019. The INDIA bloc won 56 seats as per the [results announced by the Election Commission](https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jharkhand-assembly-elections-jmm-hemant-soren-india-bloc-2638940-2024-11-23?utm_source=global-search&utm_medium=global-search&utm_campaign=global-search) on Saturday.
Now that the Soren regime is back with a bang in Jharkhand, it is surprising how the Jharkhand Mukti Morcha (JMM) chief defied all odds to lead the INDIA bloc home comfortably with a thumping majority over arch rivals NDA. Many factors and elements are believed to have paved the way for the INDIA bloc.
Soren had landed in trouble with the dawn of new year after being arrested by the Enforcement Directory (ED) on January 31 in a money laundering case.
It is believed that his arrest brought the tribals together and set the narrative that from day one after Soren was sworn in as the Chief Minister of Jharkhand, the BJP was trying to destabilise the government in the state.
The perception seems to have worked miraculously in favour of Soren and went a long way in generating a strong sympathy wave for him. The effect was quite palpable during the Lok Sabha polls as well.
The NDA lost all the 5 seats reserved for the tribals. Repeating its stellar run, the India block this time won 27 out of 28 seats reserved for the tribals, which is even better than 2019. In previous elections, the BJP lost 26 out of 28 seats reserved for Scheduled Tribes (STs). Ahead of the Assembly polls, the slogan "Jail ka badla vote se" became quite popular among the tribals.
WOMEN VOTERS SEAL FATE
The Maiayan Samman Yojna, a women's welfare scheme, is also believed to have acted as a game changer and a significant factor that turned the tide in the favour of the incumbent government. This scheme targeted at least 45 lakh beneficiaries. Under this scheme, the eligible beneficiary women were disbursed Rs 1,000 each. And the installments were doled out thrice before the polls in Jharkhand.
The minority communities, including Christians and tribals, overwhelmingly supported the INDIA bloc. Despite the NDA’s attempts to focus on religious issues, these communities trusted the INDI alliance's policies.
Their unified support played a decisive role in widening the gap between the two alliances. In the second phase that went to polls on November 20, women voters turned out to be the decisive factor in 31 seats. In 68 seats, women were the dominating voters, which sealed the fate of the election.
THE KALPANA SOREN FACTOR
Kalpna Soren, Hemant Soren's wife, also proved a big difference between the two alliances. She was a star campaigner and a crowd puller. All her meetings saw huge gatherings patiently waiting to listen to her. Her connect with the people, her ability to speak many languages, like local Sadri, Bengali, English, Hindi and Santhali, with comfort and ease, developed a sense of belonging among the voters.
Kalpana Soren spent a lot of time on the campaign trail and many believe her emergence as a key figure that has been able to fill the gap in the JMM to a large segment after party supremo Shibu Soren went out of action.
In 2019, the onus of a successful election campaign was on the shoulders of Hemant Soren. In 2024, Hemant must have heaved a sigh of relief when Kalpana Soren took over the responsibility for establishing a mass connect. It's also clear that massive campaigns and above 90 public rallies led by Kalpana also yielded a positive result for the INDIA bloc.
The waiver of the electricity bill and free power supply of 200 units also turned out to be a trump card for Hemant Soren.
NDA'S POOR SHOW
The dismal performance by the NDA flays the weak strategy as well. The BJP trusted the leaders from outside and did not utilise the local leadership to their optimal level. Assam Chief Minister Himanta Bishwa Sarma was entrusted with the responsibility of the election co-in-charge, while Shivraj Singh Chouhan was appointed as the in-charge of elections for Jharkhand.
Local issues were sidelined and the BJP campaign hovered around a single issue of infiltration. Their poor connect with the tribals once again has emerged as the biggest stumbling block on the way to success.
The BJP has lost 27 seats out of 28 reserved for the ST, implying that the tribals have outright rejected the saffron party. It also goes on to show that the BJP leadership failed to convince and persuade the tribals. Their slogan of saving the 'Adivasi Ashmita- Mati, Beti, Roti' did not hit a chord with the voters.
